title: Class 7 student attempts suicide after 'punishment' by teacher in Bengaluru
content: A Class 7 student in Bengaluru reportedly attempted suicide after a teacher's punishment. The incident occurred Tuesday, and the boy informed his sister before the act. He was reprimanded for alleged mischief and asked to leave the classroom. Police are conducting a detailed inquiry into the circumstances and any potential harassment. Parents are receiving counseling while the student receives hospital treatment.

title: Captaincy, coaching, trade talk to headline Mumbai Indians' season review next week
content: Mumbai Indians' top brass will meet in England next week for a season review that could shape the franchise's future. Captaincy, Mahela Jayawardene's coaching role and Hardik Pandya's trade situation will dominate discussions.  The five-time champions finished fourth in 2025 and a disappointing ninth in the 2026 edition.

title: Turkey's S-400 gamble: How a Gulf sale could redraw West Asia's air defence map
content: In a significant development, Turkey is exploring the sale of its Russian S-400 air defence systems to the UAE. This transaction could reshape the air defence dynamics in West Asia, potentially paving the way for Turkey's return to the F-35 program and alleviating sanctions. However, Russia's consent remains vital for this delicate arms deal amidst rising regional tensions and shifting security priorities.

title: Warren Buffet to donate his Berkshire shares to these four foundations by 2034
content: Warren Buffett plans to donate all his Berkshire shares by 2034. He will distribute these shares among four specific charitable foundations. This significant philanthropic move aims to transfer over $140 billion in wealth. The annual grants to three foundations managed by his children will grow. The Susan Thompson Buffett Foundation will receive a somewhat greater annual grant.

title: Cheaper cars & whisky: India set to win big as FTA with UK kicks in
content: With its implementation, the trade deal will become the sixth free trade agreement brought into force during the Narendra Modi government's tenure. India has previously implemented similar agreements with Mauritius, the UAE, Australia, the European Free Trade Association (EFTA) and Oman.

title: FIFA WC: Why FBI labelled Argentina vs England semi-final the 'highest-risk' match
content: England and Argentina's World Cup semifinal is the tournament's highest-risk match.  Extraordinary security measures are in place for this intense football rivalry.  Decades of political tensions and historic flashpoints fuel concerns about fan behaviour.  Officials are implementing separate entry gates to minimise potential confrontations.  Authorities are ensuring this sensitive fixture remains confined to the pitch.

title: After 18 years, Bangladesh-born author-in-exile Taslima Nasrin to return to Kolkata
content: Taslima Nasrin is making her long-anticipated return to Kolkata after an exile spanning eighteen years, forced to leave due to protests in November 2007. Overjoyed to come back to what she calls home, she will participate in a literary meet. The event features a cultural program honoring the author, accompanied by increased security to maintain order and prevent unrest.

title: Jeff Bezos’ wife Lauren Sánchez leads $100M investment to transform 8 US cities
content: Lauren Sanchez Bezos is directing significant Bezos Earth Fund investments. The fund is transforming urban spaces into green community hubs. This initiative aims to connect people and nature within cities. The Bezos Earth Fund also supports marine protection and AI climate solutions. Overall, the fund continues its commitment to climate and nature initiatives.

title: Agni, Akash, Prithvi & more: How a 1983 Kalam‑led project forged India's missile mastery
content: India's Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme began in 1983. This initiative aimed for domestic missile technology and strategic autonomy. Five distinct missile projects were conceived to meet operational needs. These projects transformed India into a credible missile manufacturer. The programme achieved significant technological independence in missile development.

title: Britain bought lifeless farmland for 13.8 million Euros, now beavers help it earn
content: A farm in Lincolnshire is evolving into a lucrative wildland endeavor, with beavers playing a pivotal role in this ecological project. The initiative focuses on demonstrating that restoring nature can lead to substantial private income. Current revenue streams are generated through carbon removal and biodiversity credits, with aspirations to draw significant investments for sustainable environmental solutions.

title: Your personality may decide which workout you enjoy most, study says
content: New research suggests personality traits influence exercise enjoyment and adherence. Extraverted individuals often prefer high-intensity interval training sessions. Conscientious people tend to engage in more regular physical activity and structured programs. Those with neurotic tendencies may benefit from shorter, intense workouts for stress reduction. Understanding personal preferences can guide effective and sustainable fitness choices.

title: OpenAI's first device rumoured to be a ChatGPT-powered speaker that moves
content: Everyone braced for an OpenAI phone—maybe even a pendant or a pen. Instead, the ChatGPT maker's first hardware device is a portable, screenless smart speaker that physically moves to feel "alive," per Bloomberg. It's the first of roughly five devices planned, so those other form factors could still come. Running on ChatGPT, it learns your habits and acts before you ask—but Apple's trade-secret lawsuit clouds the 2027 launch.

title: Man's fast ruined by fungus-contaminated juice; court awards Rs 70,000
content: A Himachal Pradesh consumer commission found Dabur India liable for selling contaminated juice. The company must pay one lakh rupees for compensation and costs. A man discovered fungus in his Real Fruit Power Mosambi juice pack. He suffered emotional and spiritual distress while observing a religious fast. The commission cited manufacturing defects and negligence in its ruling.

title: Six times, same result! Yamal continues to be Mbappe's knockout nemesis
content: Lamine Yamal continued his flawless knockout streak, overcoming Kylian Mbappé as Spain triumphed over France 2-0 in the FIFA World Cup 2026 semifinal. This impressive win guarantees Spain a spot in the finals. Remarkably, Yamal has now achieved six consecutive victories against Mbappé in knockout rounds, showcasing the young talent's resilience in high-stakes matches.

title: ‘If you don’t want E20, you can buy 100% petrol, but pay more’: Nitin Gadkari
content: Union minister Nitin Gadkari denied personal gain from the ethanol blending program. He stated that allegations of conflict of interest are politically motivated and lack substance. Gadkari emphasized his long-standing support for alternative fuels to reduce pollution and import dependence. He clarified that his sons' business has a minimal share in the ethanol sector. The minister also highlighted the program's historical backing from previous governments.

title: NHL trade rumor: Patrick Kane linked to Edmonton Oilers in free agency
content: Patrick Kane is a potential free agent target for the Edmonton Oilers.  The veteran winger is reportedly considering signing with a fourth NHL franchise.  Detroit Red Wings attempted to retain Kane with a contract offer.  Edmonton has cap space and seeks to strengthen its roster for 2026-27.  Kane brings championship experience and offensive prowess to contenders.

title: Removing woman's salwar, pressing her chest not attempt to rape: Patna HC acquits man
content: The Patna High Court acquitted a man convicted of attempt to rape and wrongful confinement.  The court found his actions insufficient to establish the serious charge of attempt to rape.  Evidence presented did not meet the legal threshold for such a severe offense.  The judges noted a lack of medical corroboration for the allegations.  Consequently, the man was cleared of all charges by the high court.

title: Watch: Rohit's 'nataraja shot' leaves DK in awe during 1st ODI vs England
content: In a thrilling start to the ODI series, India outperformed England to clinch a remarkable win. The visitors showcased their batting prowess by chasing down the target with plenty of balls to spare. Rohit Sharma's stunning pull shot became a highlight of the match, drawing admiration and reminding fans of his signature play. This victory also ended England's impressive unbeaten run at Edgbaston.

title: 'Received nothing but silence': CJP chief slams Centre as Wangchuk's fast enters Day 18
content: Abhijeet Dipke criticized the government's silence regarding Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike. Wangchuk has been fasting for eighteen days protesting examination irregularities and paper leaks. He questioned why the Prime Minister has not engaged in dialogue. Opposition leaders have appealed to Wangchuk to end his fast due to his health. Wangchuk has lost 8.5 kg since beginning his protest on June 28.

title: Gujarat shocker: Lawyer assisting in 13-year-old’s rape case accused of raping her
content: A lawyer allegedly raped a 13-year-old girl he was assisting with a prior case.  He offered legal help in exchange for domestic work due to the family's financial distress.  The girl was forced into sexual acts and then threatened by the lawyer.  Police arrested the lawyer and booked him under relevant sections of the BNS and Pocso Act.  Further investigation is currently underway to collect all necessary evidence.

title: 'If she publicly says ...': Cong extends Martyrs' Day invite to Mamata, but with a condition
content: The Congress invited Mamata Banerjee to its Martyrs' Day event in Kolkata. This invitation came with a condition that she acknowledge leaving Congress was a mistake. West Bengal Congress president stated Mamata should not distort the historical significance of the 1993 movement. He added that admitting her past decision was wrong would be a significant gesture. The Congress stage remains open for her to pay respects to the martyrs.

title: Scientists may have solved 252 mn-year-old mystery behind Earth's greatest mass extinction
content: A new study reveals metabolism's role in Earth's greatest marine extinction event.  Organisms' oxygen use and energy generation dictated survival during this ancient crisis.  Warming oceans and declining oxygen levels stressed less active ancient marine life.  More mobile animals with higher energy demands better survived deteriorating conditions.  This ancient shift continues to shape today's diverse ocean ecosystems.

title: I'm ready for India captaincy, says Smriti Mandhana
content: Smriti Mandhana reflected on India's historic Lord's Test victory and her significant contributions. She also discussed lessons learned from past T20 World Cup disappointments. The impact of the Women's Premier League on Indian talent was also a key topic. Mandhana expressed her readiness to lead India if the captaincy opportunity arises. She highlighted the importance of a positive team culture in her leadership philosophy.

title: Road to SA up and running: Washington, Axar deliver with bat and ball at Edgbaston
content: Axar Patel and Washington Sundar rescued India from 160/4 with an unbeaten 102-run stand to seal a six-wicket win over England in the first ODI at Edgbaston. The partnership not only ended India's winless run in the UK but also highlighted Gautam Gambhir's evolving batting blueprint ahead of the 2027 ODI World Cup.

title: B'luru professor gets one-line termination notice after 19 years of service; fights back
content: An assistant professor was reinstated after nearly two decades of service at Jain College.  Her termination notice was issued without proper authority and due process.  The college claimed declining enrollment and syllabus changes due to NEP 2020.  However, evidence showed her teaching load had significantly increased.  The tribunal ruled the termination was not based on genuine administrative necessity.

title: Diego Maradona and Lionel Messi: Twin legacies, intertwined yet distinct
content: Lionel Messi overcame significant national team lows to achieve ultimate glory. He finally secured major titles with Argentina after years of personal frustration. His journey mirrors Diego Maradona's iconic status in Argentine football history. Manager Lionel Scaloni fostered a team that plays for Messi. This collective effort has redefined Messi's legacy for a new generation.

title: Aadhaar card not reliable proof of age in accident compensation cases: Chhattisgarh HC
content: The high court ruled insurance liability begins with policy issuance, not premium payment. An insurer cannot be held liable for accidents occurring before the policy's effective date. The tribunal's reliance on an Aadhaar card for age determination was deemed incorrect by the court. Compensation amounts were significantly enhanced for victims and deceased individuals in connected claims. The court dismissed appeals from the driver and owner while partially allowing claimant appeals.

title: When R Madhavan spoke about Bollywood actors not getting residuals as in Hollywood
content: Bollywood actors navigate a precarious financial landscape due to the absence of a residual payment system, a stark contrast to Hollywood’s lucrative model. R Madhavan pointed out that earnings from merely three films in Hollywood can secure wealth for generations. Many actors maintain lavish lifestyles while often overlooking the vulnerability of their income streams, leading them to prioritize commercial endeavors for quick cash.

title: Need to relook if English can be considered an indigenous language: SC
content: The Supreme Court questioned English's indigenous status while refusing to halt the CBSE's three-language scheme. This policy aims to promote Hindi and regional languages, aligning with constitutional goals. Petitioners argued against the scheme due to teacher and infrastructure challenges. They also noted limited availability of books on the NCERT website. The Centre and CBSE will file their responses within ten days.

title: India a 'huge growth opportunity': Investment pact with UK can boost FDI
content: India presents significant global investment opportunities, attracting companies with its long-term growth potential. A proposed Bilateral Investment Treaty between India and the UK could boost investor confidence. The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement offers preferential market access for exports. Discussions continue for a Bilateral Investment Treaty to safeguard future investments. This treaty aims to strengthen the broader economic partnership between both nations.

title: 'Won’t quit, Ayodhya Ram temple donation theft due to SBI laxity': Trust treasurer
content: Swami Govinddev Giri Maharaj stated he will not resign as treasurer of the trust. He believes bank officials should have flagged the alleged donation embezzlement. The trust's general secretary Champat Rai recently resigned following the case. Giri Maharaj acknowledged security lapses and promised system strengthening measures. He estimated the embezzlement amount to be around Rs3 crore.

title: Exclusive! Jennifer Winget to get married tomorrow
content: Actress Jennifer Winget is set to marry William Ishmael this Thursday. The intimate wedding ceremony will take place in the United Kingdom. Sources confirm a private white wedding with close family and friends attending. Jennifer previously married Karan Singh Grover, which ended within two years. She has since focused on her successful acting career in television and OTT.

title: Did Lamine Yamal get away with handball? Why Spain's penalty stood against France
content: Spain's 2-0 World Cup semi-final win over France was sparked by a controversial penalty awarded after Lamine Yamal was fouled by Lucas Digne. Claims of a possible handball in the build-up dominated debate, but rules experts explained the contact was not punishable under IFAB laws. Despite Didier Deschamps questioning the officiating, the decision was ultimately deemed correct.

title: FBI adds Indian-origin man to most wanted list over alleged organised crime links
content: The FBI has added Nitish Kaushal to its most wanted list for his alleged role in organized crime. Kaushal is linked to the Jaggu Bhagwanpuria group involved in various illegal activities. This action follows charges against Lawrence Bishnoi and Goldy Brar for a Canadian assassination. Federal agents recently conducted large raids targeting the Lawrence Bishnoi network in the United States.

title: IBM's 'ugly moment': Worst fall since 1987, $69 bn wiped out — Why did stock crash 25%?
content: ​​Shares of IBM plunged 25% on Tuesday, with the company seeing a steeper one-day decline than it experienced during the 1987 "Black Monday" market crash. The weakness also spread to other software stocks.

title: DHSE Kerala plus one result to be released today: Check complete details here
content: The Directorate of Higher Secondary Education (DHSE), Kerala, is expected to declare the Plus One (Class 11) Result 2026 on July 15. Students can access their scorecards online using their registration number and date of birth. Candidates must secure at least 35 per cent marks to qualify for promotion, while unsuccessful students can appear for the SAY/Improvement examinations.

title: Watch: Riots erupt in France after 0-2 FIFA World Cup semifinal defeat to Spain
content: France's 2026 FIFA World Cup journey concluded with a semifinal loss to Spain. Viral social media claims of widespread riots across France following the defeat . Thousands of disappointed fans gathered in public squares and fan zones after the match. Spain secured a 2-0 victory, ending France's hopes of a third World Cup title. The team's attacking cohesion and Kylian Mbappé's performance were key discussion points.

title: US eases Russia sanctions bill, cuts tariff threat on India and China from 500% to 100%
content: US senators unveiled a revised Russia sanctions bill targeting major energy buyers. This legislation seeks to pressure Moscow and its key oil and gas importers. The updated bill reduces previously proposed tariffs on top purchasers like China and India. It also targets Russia's shadow fleet and financial institutions involved in its war. This measure aims to secure broader political support and honor Senator Lindsey Graham's legacy.

title: NBA trade rumor: Bronny James could rival LeBron James after Lakers move
content: Bronny James remains under a guaranteed contract with the Los Angeles Lakers for the 2026-27 season despite his father LeBron James's confirmed departure from the franchise after eight years. The Athletic's Dan Woike reports the Lakers consider Bronny's roster future independent of LeBron's free agency decision, pointing to his inclusion in the team's veteran minicamp as evidence. While rival executives have discussed Bronny as a potential incentive to help recruit LeBron, no trade has been reported, and the Lakers' own front-office moves suggest they intend to keep developing him.

title: India end England's 12-year home stronghold at Edgbaston with six-wicket win
content: India defeated England by six wickets at Edgbaston on Tuesday, July 14, 2026. England's strong start faltered after reaching sixty-one without loss. Axar Patel's four wickets helped restrict England to two hundred fifty-eight runs. Shubman Gill scored eighty runs, and Axar Patel added fifty-seven not out. India achieved the target with twenty-eight deliveries remaining in the match.

title: Gangster Rohit Godara's Home Razed Overnight, Cops Say 'We Didn't Do It'
content: Preliminary assessment suggested that an earthmover machine was used to bring down a considerable part of the building, officials said.

title: "Fastest Implementation": UK Trade Minister On Trade Deal With India
content: Chris Bryant said deal marks quickest turnaround UK has ever managed between signing an agreement and bringing it into effect.

title: India-UK Trade Deal Kicks In: What Gets Cheaper, Which Sectors Gain Most
content: India-UK FTA: For Indian consumers, imported Scotch whisky, premium British cars, cosmetics, chocolates and select food products could get cheaper.

title: Taslima Nasreen's Kolkata Return Sparks Fresh Political Firestorm
content: Taslima Nasreen is scheduled to attend a programme protesting fundamentalism at Rabindra Sadan in Kolkata on August 1.

title: Fake IAS Officer Assaults Husband, Demands 40 Lakhs To 'Build' Hospital
content: The accused posed as an IAS officer through fake social media to marry Abhishek. Later, strangulated him and demanded Rs 40 lakh as extortion.

title: UP Medical University Bans Cooking Non-Veg In Hostels After Governor Visit
content: KGMU bans preparation of non-vegetarian food in hostels after concerns raised by UP Governor Anandiben Patel.

title: On Run Since 2025, Criminal Posing As 'Peer Baba' Arrested By Hyderabad Cops
content: During the operation, police seized 115 grams of dry ganja (Cannabis) and the Peer Baba attire allegedly used by the accused to avoid detection.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: Iran Threatens To Shut More Energy Corridors After Hormuz Closure
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: A fifth of all traded crude oil and natural gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the war, when it was open to all without tolls.

title: Stones Thrown, Vehicles Torched: Mob Attack At Assam Rifles Camp In Manipur
content: The mob reached the Assam Rifles Company Operating Base (COB) at 9:30 pm and reportedly starting throwing stones, and damaging property.

title: "Ship Has Safely Crossed": Indian Man's Last Text To Family Before Strike Killed Him
content: Karmarkar, 30, was among the Indian crew members who had gone missing following an attack on the merchant vessel 'GFX Galaxy' near the coast of Oman in the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday.

title: Relief For India, China As US Lowers 500% Russian Oil Tariff Threat To 100%
content: The updated version also allows an exception for countries that import less than 15 per cent of Russia's natural gas exports and are taking significant steps to reduce those imports, which could exempt Japan, France, Hungary, and Belgium.

title: OpenAI's First Device To Be Movable, Screenless Speaker To Challenge Apple
content: OpenAI is developing a movable, screen-free AI speaker as its first hardware product, positioning it to rival Apple amid an ongoing trade secret lawsuit

title: Centre Orders Real-Time Monitoring Of Indian Seafarers Amid Gulf Attacks
content: Government orders real-time tracking of Indian seafarers in Gulf after Strait of Hormuz attacks left one dead, nine injured on merchant vessels

title: Supreme Court Pulls Up Patna High Court Over 'Grabbing Breast Not Rape Attempt' Order
content: The Patna High Court recently held that removing a woman's salwar and pressing her chest is not sufficient to prove an attempt to rape

title: 'Death Row Can Be Revised': Bangladesh 'Welcomes' Sheikh Hasina's Return Plan
content: Last week, sources close to Hasina said she was preparing to voluntarily return to Dhaka by the end of this year to revive her Awami League party.

title: Bengaluru Sees Hottest July In 112 Years, Mercury Hits 33.6 Degree Celsius
content: According to the Meteorological Department, the previous highest July temperature in Bengaluru was 33.3 degree Celsius, recorded in 1914 and again in 1926.

title: 'If Missile Hits Supertanker Cargo': Indian Sailor's Worst Fear In Strait Of Hormuz
content: Indian sailors who steered supertankers in the Strait of Hormuz narrate the challenges of keeping alive the complex system of transport that holds the world together, but at a great cost to their own safety and lives

title: Draft Report On June 2025 Air India Crash Due In October: Probe Body
content: Draft final report on June 2025 Air India crash expected in October, Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau tells Supreme Court.

title: No Turning Back The Clock? US House Passes Daylight Saving Time Bill
content: Daylight saving time is that period between spring and fall when clocks in most parts of the United States are set one hour ahead of standard time.

title: Musk May Have Broken Wisconsin Election Law By Offering $1 Million To Voters
content: A bipartisan Wisconsin panel found that Elon Musk may have violated state election laws by offering $1 million payments to voters during the 2025 Supreme Court race.

title: India–UK CETA comes into force; Telangana well poised to reap benefits
content: Pharmaceuticals and Life Sciences, electronics and precision engineering, engineering goods and industrial machinery  and chemicals and specialty chemicals are set to benefit most from the landmark agreement that came into force on July 15

title: Daughter turned away from Mudragada’s bier; State honours for last rites today
content: B. Kranthi, who had campaigned against her father’s party in 2024, was escorted away by the police amid slogans from family members and supporters at Kirlampudi

title: Matrimonial disputes: Kerala HC says courts must not assume bride entrusted gold based on social customs
content: HC says courts must look for oral, documentary or circumstantial evidence which can be used to infer if gold or money was entrusted to the husband or his relatives, or was misappropriated by them

title: Bihar CM Samrat Choudhary inaugurates State Level Sahyog Programme
content: Mr. Choudhary said the state government is working with a resolve to serve the public, and ensuring an impartial resolution for every grievance is the government’s top priority.

title: Mob vandalises Assam Rifles camp, torches vehicles after search operation conducted in Manipur
content: A Defence spokesperson says the incident occurred after an area domination patrol and search operation near a designated NSCN (I-M) camp

title: Jaishankar meets European Council president, values his role in advancing trade, technology cooperation
content: The External Affairs Minister is on a two-day visit to Belgium from July 14 to 15 to participate in the 3rd India-EU Trade and Technology Council meeting and interact with his EU and Belgian counterparts in Brussels

title: Two law students arrested for hurling abuses in Supreme Court, disrupting proceedings
content: According to police, the incident occurred during the hearing of Special Leave Petition (Civil), Prabal Pratap and Another vs State of Uttar Pradesh through Commissioner inside Court No. 13 of the apex court on July 10

title: Speculations abuzz after NCP, NCP SP leaders meet CM Fadnavis
content: Amid speculations that the majority of NCP SP legislators want to go with the BJP, the possibility of a merger between NCP and NCP SP is also being discussed in hush-hushed tones

title: What are the gains from the India-U.K. trade deal? | Explained
content: Zero tariffs. Cheaper whisky and British cars. Major benefits for Indian workers in the U.K. The India-U.K. trade deal could reshape business on both sides, and here’s the complete picture

title: Kerala govt plans PPP model for J.C. Daniel international film city proposed in revised Budget
content: Government considering multiple locations in Ernakulam to establish its ambitious venture aimed at creating a one-stop destination for film shooting

title: Who is Anil Menon, the Indian-origin NASA astronaut of Malayali descent now in Earth orbit?
content: Anil Menon, NASA’s first astronaut of Malayali origin, embarks on a historic ISS mission, conducting groundbreaking research in space.

title: For justice around the clock | Explained
content: The Supreme Court has decided to examine a write petition for a framework to urgently set up Benches; the petition argues that in a system where executive power may operate at any hour, the protection of fundamental rights cannot remain constrained by the temporal boundaries of court schedules

title: From Kerala to latex: Tracing London-based designer Harri KS’s unlikely rise
content: Raised around rubber plantations in Kerala, designer Harri KS is rethinking latex, form, and the idea of wearability

title: When debris came crashing down: How rains and undisposed mud led to a disaster in Wayanad
content: A devastating mudslide in Wayanad, Kerala, triggered by heavy rains and poor construction practices, has resulted in multiple casualties

title: Zubeen Garg’s last recorded duet with predeceased co-singer released
content: The song, ‘Xorogi Xopun’, is from the Assamese feature film Dipholu, to be released in theatres on August 21, 2026

title: West Bengal government seeks support of DMSC for cervical cancer vaccination for sex workers in Sonagachi
content: Durbar Mahila Samanwaya Committee, a collective for working on rights and recognition of sex workers, celebrates 31 years

title: Can a person be declared a foreigner without a fair hearing? | Explained
content: The case arose out of proceedings before the Foreigners Tribunals in the State of Assam and, in certain cases, before the erstwhile Illegal Migrants (Determination) Tribunals

title: Five decades of Shipra Bhattacharya’s art come together in Kolkata retrospective
content: ‘In Bloom’ in Kolkata traces the artist’s evolving engagement with womanhood, memory and the human condition through paintings, drawings and sculptures

title: Silk saree that fits into a matchbox presented to Goddess Bharamaraba
content: The saree measures 5.5 metres in length and 48 inches in width and weighs approximately 200 grams; designed in the traditional “Ikkat” pattern, the saree can easily fit into a small matchbox when folded, the weaver explained

title: Gangster Rohit Godara's ancestral home demolished overnight, police begin probe
content: According to police, the demolition took place at a time when no one was present at the property in Loonkaransar town

title: This artist is documenting Bengaluru’s mango season in the face of climate change and ecological loss
content: Using ASCII typographic symbols and 3D scans, Shruti Nagaraj’s project Maavu maps the cycle of growth, consumption, and decay of mango varieties. The project was showcased in Saatchi Gallery in London and there are plans to come to India soon

title: Kejriwal to visit CJP protest at Jantar Mantar on July 16
content: "Sonam Wangchuk has been on a hunger strike for some days, and his health is deteriorating. He is an asset of the country, and I appeal to him to end his hunger strike. There are other ways also for the struggle," says Arvind Kejriwal

title: Centre bans paraquat dichloride, a herbicide linked to thousands of self-harm, homicide and accidental deaths
content: India joins the long list of over 70 countries that have banned the toxic herbicide which has no antidote

title: ‘Donate a Memory’: Bengaluru-based Nightingales Medical Trust seeks vintage household items for India’s first integrated dementia care village
content: The Trust said familiar objects from earlier decades can serve as powerful memory cues for people with dementia, helping stimulate recognition, encourage conversations, reduce anxiety and improve emotional well-being.

title: Oil exploration only after resolving Assam-Nagaland border disputes: Konyak Union
content: The apex body of the Konyak Nagas says territorial clarity, landowners’ consent, and stronger border security must precede implementation of the bilateral agreement

title: Amended RPwD Act to include persons internally injured by acid ingestion, Centre tells Supreme Court
content: The 2016 law had previously recognised only victims of acid-throwing and not forcible acid ingestion; the amendment will have a retrospective effect

title: Kerala HC directs Defence Ministry to file report on hazardous cargo threat from MSC Elsa 3 shipwreck
content: Assessing the survey reports, the court noted that approximately 84 containers were partially embedded in the seabed, and several had structural damage

title: Project to fill 88 tanks in Haveri district will be helpful in rain-deficit years, says Karnataka’s PWD Minister Satish Jarkiholi
content: PWD Minister Satish Jarkiholi says the government of Karnataka has chalked out a plan to construct check dams on Tunga and Varada rivers in the district, which would help in improving the ground water table

title: Why Pune’s cafés of the moment are built for slowing down
content: Young restaurateurs are creating community spaces with run clubs, coffee raves and workshops. Here, lingering is encouraged, coffee mornings give way to cocktail nights, and globally inspired menus define Pune’s new slow-living café culture.

title: Land acquisition from tribal council area for Guwahati expansion project opposed
content: The local units of the Bodo and Rabha students’ bodies say the proposed acquisition would violate the provisions of the Rabha Hasong Autonomous Council Act and adversely affect tribal land rights

title: Nashik family outing turns violent; nine detained
content: Nine individuals detained after a violent altercation during a Nashik family outing near Bhavli Dam waterfall

title: Police’s illegal entry into woman's bedroom amounts to violation of privacy: Bombay Hign Court
content: The bench observed that while monetary compensation cannot fully redress the invasion of privacy and dignity, it would provide some measure of solace and serve as a reminder that investigative powers must be exercised in accordance with law

title: How new tehsils, districts for Ladakh address political aspirations? | Explained
content: Why is the Centre rolling out new measures to Ladakh? What is LAHDC Act? What is the need for new districts and tehsils?

title: Amid wrangle, LDF lays groundwork for CPI-CPI(M) detente over Kerala Deputy Opposition Leader post
content: T.P. Ramakrishnan, who held a brief closed-door meeting with the CPI State Secretary Binoy Viswam, signalled that the rapprochement was not immediately at hand. He said the discussions were in their nascent stages and denied any rift in the coalition. Viswam merely stated that “CPI and CPI(M) pursued the same politics”

title: No more Chemburs: How can Indian cities ensure their trees do not ‘turn against’ their people
content: Even as the verdict of the inquiry committee in Chembur tree fall incident comes in, a stroll through three other Indian metros, Chennai, Bengaluru and Delhi, to find out if they are proactive enough with their trees to prevent a Chembur on their soil

title: Is the undocumented migrant counted?
content: Contrary to the claims that there could be 20 million “illegal Bangladeshi migrants” staying in India, an analysis based on Census data suggests that there is no surge of undocumented migrants from Bangladesh, at least between 1991 and 2011

title: CM Naidu calls for reforms in business rules, faster governance through technology
content: Naidu instructs officials to constitute a committee to redraft the Business Rules and introduce reforms that would make governance more efficient and responsive

title: West Bengal govt allocates 1,000 km of land to set up fence of about 172 km along international border
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said that his government has expedited the land transfer process to the Border Security Force (BSF) for the construction of essential fencing.

title: Norway firm to set up India’s first river kinetic energy demo plant in Arunachal
content: Science and Technology Minister Dasanglu Pul says the technology to generate electricity from river currents without requiring major civil infrastructure could ensure environmentally responsible utilisation of natural resources

title: Ram Temple corruption episode biggest proof of BJP govt. failure: U.P. Congress chief Ajay Rai
content: ‘When it comes to taking credit for the temple, Prime Minister Narendra Modi and U.P. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath are always at the forefront; hence they will be blamed for this heinous crime and theft at the temple,’ said Mr. Rai

title: Dr. Mustafa Kamal, the Abdullah who rooted for J&K’s pre-1953 position, dies in Srinagar
content: Mustafa Kamal would blame the Congress for embroiling the Kashmir issue by arresting his father and party founder Sheikh Muhammad Abdullah in 1953

title: India blames Pakistan for protests in PoK, seeks accountability
content: “We expect and hope that the international community will hold Pakistan fully accountable for egregious abuses and misdeeds,” says MEA spokesperson

title: Supreme Court says call records alone not cogent proof, clears woman of husband’s murder
content: Mere production of telephone records does not substitute substantive proof of an illicit affair leading to murder, the top court said in the case dating back to 2007

title: Can’t English be considered an indigenous Indian language, asks Supreme Court
content: SC questions the use of the colonially loaded term ‘native’ in CBSE circular, says the push for three-language scheme may be in the spirit of the Constitutional goal to use Indian languages for official purposes

title: Former SCERT Director arrested over errors in Odisha textbooks
content: Acts and omissions resulted in the publication and distribution of erroneous textbooks, causing wrongful loss to the State exchequer of approximately ₹175 crore, says Crime Branch

title: CJP protest Day 25: As Sonam Wangchuk’s condition deteriorates, leaders urge him to end hunger strike
content: Over 1,800 “writers, academics, social activists and other concerned citizens” signed on to an online petition asking Mr. Wangchuk to end his fast.

title: Odisha panel clears early release of prime convict in murder of Graham Staines
content: Odisha State Sentence Review Board has recommended Dara Singh’s release on the grounds of ‘good behaviour’

title: 'Hormuz to remain closed until end of America's evils': Iran defiant as US continues strikes
